wafv2.webacl.responseinspectionjson.CfnA:
Configures inspection of the response JSON. AWS WAF can inspect the first 65,536 bytes (64 KB) of the response JSON. This is part of the `ResponseInspection` configuration for `AWSManagedRulesATPRuleSet` and `AWSManagedRulesACFPRuleSet`. 

**Note**  
Response inspection is available only in web ACLs that protect Amazon CloudFront distributions.

wafv2.webacl.responseinspectionjson.Fail.Description: Values for the specified identifier in the response JSON that indicate a failed login or account creation attempt. To be counted as a failure, the value must be an exact match, including case. Each value must be unique among the success and failure values.   JSON example: `"FailureValues": [ "False", "Failed" ]`

wafv2.webacl.responseinspectionjson.Iden.Description: The identifier for the value to match against in the JSON. The identifier must be an exact match, including case.  JSON examples: `"Identifier": [ "/login/success" ]` and `"Identifier": [ "/sign-up/success" ]`

wafv2.webacl.responseinspectionjson.Succ.Description: Values for the specified identifier in the response JSON that indicate a successful login or account creation attempt. To be counted as a success, the value must be an exact match, including case. Each value must be unique among the success and failure values.   JSON example: `"SuccessValues": [ "True", "Succeeded" ]`
